# Cross-model and cross-harness experiment plan

*Generated by research-copilot. Plans the smallest set of additional
experiments that would let the paper claim "this finding generalises
across base models and across coding harnesses".*

## What we have today

| dimension | what we ran | what we did not |
|---|---|---|
| base model | \texttt{deepseek-v4-flash} on all cells (test-17 and Held-out-10) | every other model on the cells; \texttt{deepseek-v3.2} and \texttt{minimax-m2.7} on plugin-only |
| harness | Claude Code (CC) only | OpenHands, OpenCode (any other) |

We have a single backbone × single harness Cartesian point. The reviewer-relevant question is whether the X+M / SE story is a deepseek-v4-flash-on-CC artefact. We need cross-model and cross-harness panels to answer it.

## What to actually run (priority-ordered)

The full Cartesian product (every cell × every model × every harness × 3 seeds) is far too expensive. The smallest set of experiments that gives reviewers what they want is:

1. **Cross-model on CC**: \emph{Vanilla}, \emph{X+M}, \emph{SE} cells × {\texttt{minimax-m2.7}, \texttt{google/gemini-3-flash-preview}} × 3 seeds × test-17.
   - 3 cells × 2 models × 3 seeds × 17 tasks = 306 task-runs.
   - Optionally also × Held-out-10 (10 tasks) = +180 task-runs.
   - Decision: include Held-out-10 only if test-17 numbers are not catastrophic (≥0.6). The hard-tail story replicates only if the model can do *anything* on Held-out-10.

2. **Cross-harness on \texttt{deepseek-v4-flash}**: \emph{Vanilla}, \emph{X+M}, \emph{SE} cells × {OpenHands, OpenCode} × 3 seeds × test-17.
   - 3 cells × 2 harnesses × 3 seeds × 17 tasks = 306 task-runs.
   - Optionally also × Held-out-10 = +180 task-runs.

Total core budget: 612 task-runs at $\sim$5 minutes each (fast model) = ~50 GPU-hours equivalent; at API costs assume $\sim$$0.01 per task-run on the cheap models = $\sim$$6. Adding Held-out-10 costs another ~$3. The cross-harness numbers may be more expensive if OpenHands/OpenCode add per-run overhead.

Larger cross-product (full factorial cells × all models × all harnesses) is not justified; we are asking ``does the X+M/SE finding generalise'', not ``what is the global Pareto frontier''.

## Cross-model details

\paragraph{minimax-m2.7 via OpenRouter.}
We previously had \texttt{minimax-m2.7} integrated; the rate-limit and timeout knobs are known. Provider-side determinism is partial: OpenRouter does not expose a seed flag, so ``seeds'' are independent runs with identical prompts (same convention as \texttt{deepseek-v4-flash}).

\paragraph{google/gemini-3-flash-preview via OpenRouter.}
This is the same model we used for offline distillation of the m1u cheatsheet. Running it as the inference model is a reasonable cross-check (does the cheatsheet still help even when the inference model is the one that produced it offline?). On preflight, gemini-flash on OpenRouter has had high-latency / 1-tool-per-minute issues; we should run a smoke first and bail if a single test-17 task takes over $\sim$15 minutes.

\paragraph{What the result space looks like.}
Three plausible patterns:
1. \emph{Adapter ranking is preserved} (X+M $\geq$ SE $\geq$ Vanilla on each model): the strongest result; supports a generic claim.
2. \emph{Adapter ranking is preserved on hard tasks but tied on easy tasks}: the most likely outcome given the hard-tail finding; still supports the claim with a caveat.
3. \emph{Adapter ranking inverts on a different model}: an interesting negative result; supports a more careful claim about which adaptations transfer.

We design the analysis to detect all three.

## Cross-harness details

\paragraph{OpenHands.}
We have prior integration. Two open questions before re-using it:
\begin{itemize}
  \item Did we resolve the DeepSeek-API integration issue (the openai-compatible endpoint configuration that broke last time)? Action: run a smoke on a single task with vanilla OpenHands + DSv4-flash, verify it produces an XML and the trajectory is recorded.
  \item Have our hooks/MCP integration assumptions changed? OpenHands does not expose a Stop-hook in the same way Claude Code does. We need to either (a) port the parse-check + xmllint validation as a post-write callback inside the OpenHands action loop, or (b) run a Vanilla-equivalent only and skip S/X cells on OpenHands.
\end{itemize}

For minimum coverage, run \emph{Vanilla} (no plugin, no hook, no MCP, no cheatsheet) and \emph{X+M-equivalent} (the agent-callable validator MCP + memory cheatsheet appended to the system prompt) on OpenHands. Skip SE on OpenHands for now; SE depends on Claude Code's plugin packaging.

\paragraph{OpenCode.}
We have no integration yet. Required work before any experiment:
\begin{enumerate}
  \item Identify how OpenCode handles agent-callable tools (MCP, function-calling, custom tools) and how to inject a memory cheatsheet into its system prompt.
  \item Build a thin wrapper that exposes our retrieval and validator tools to OpenCode in whatever interface it natively accepts.
  \item Reproduce parse-check + xmllint validation as a post-write hook (the same logic as our Claude Code hook, just wrapped for OpenCode).
\end{enumerate}
Smoketest first on a single task to confirm the harness actually exits cleanly with XML on disk before launching the 3-cell sweep.

\paragraph{What the result space looks like.}
The cross-harness result is more sensitive to whether other harnesses naturally produce file-system output. Vanilla CC reaches $0.910$ on test-17 partly because CC reliably writes files. Other harnesses might score lower than $0.910$ even at Vanilla --- that is itself a finding (``the harness matters before any GEOS-specific adaptation does''). The most important thing to report is the relative $\Delta$ from Vanilla to X+M / SE within each harness, not the absolute fa0.

## Pre-conditions before launching

\begin{itemize}
  \item Retest Vanilla \texttt{deepseek-v4-flash} CC results (control). If our numbers reproduce within seed noise, the rest of the campaign is on solid ground.
  \item Smoketest each new (model, harness) combination on a single test-17 task before launching 3 seeds × 17. Bail if the smoketest fails or takes $>$15 minutes.
  \item For each cell × model × harness combination, capture the same metadata as our autocamp campaign (tool counts, wall time, trajectory features) so the cross-model/harness panels are directly comparable to the headline.
\end{itemize}

## Decision rule for what to include in the paper

\begin{enumerate}
  \item Always include cross-model R-factor verification (\texttt{deepseek-v3.2}, \texttt{minimax-m2.7}); we already have this.
  \item Include cross-model X+M and SE only if at least one new model produces meaningful Held-out-10 numbers. Otherwise note in limitations that we were unable to verify cross-model.
  \item Include cross-harness Vanilla and X+M-equivalent on OpenHands as a panel-of-three (CC, OpenHands, OpenCode). If OpenCode integration is too heavy, drop it and frame as ``CC vs OpenHands'' with a clear note about why OpenCode is missing.
  \item Hard pause: if a cross-harness Vanilla run produces fa0 $\leq 0.4$, that is a harness-level capability finding worth reporting in its own paragraph, not a flaw in our adapter design.
\end{enumerate>

## Open questions for the user before launching

\begin{enumerate}
  \item Is there capacity to do both cross-model and cross-harness in this campaign, or should we prioritise one? My recommendation: cross-model is cheaper and more directly attacks the ``single backbone'' limitation; cross-harness is more publication-strong but more work.
  \item Should we drop OpenCode if integration is non-trivial? My recommendation: yes, drop OpenCode for the v1 submission and add it as a v2 follow-up.
  \item Do we want to re-test gemini-3-flash-preview as the inference model given that we used it for offline distillation? My recommendation: yes, it is a useful self-check on the cheatsheet's transferability.
\end{enumerate}

## Concrete launcher sketch

We can extend the existing factorial-launcher infrastructure:

```bash
# scripts/launch_cross_model.sh
for MODEL in deepseek-v4-flash minimax-m2.7 google-gemini-3-flash-preview; do
  for CELL in autocamp_F0 autocamp_F4 autocamp_SE; do  # Vanilla, X+M, SE
    for SEED in 1 2 3; do
      run_seed "$CELL" "$SEED" "$MODEL" --task-set test-17
    done
  done
done

# scripts/launch_cross_harness.sh
for HARNESS in claude-code openhands opencode; do
  for CELL in vanilla x-plus-m; do
    for SEED in 1 2 3; do
      run_harness "$HARNESS" "$CELL" "$SEED" --task-set test-17
    done
  done
done
```

The factorial-launcher already supports per-cell config; we add a `--model` flag and a `--harness` selector that swap in different runner modules. The OpenHands and OpenCode runners need to be written from scratch (or the OpenHands one updated if the integration was last seen pre-DSv4).

## What I will not do until the user weighs in

- Spawn the actual experiment runs. Cross-model is cheap but not free; cross-harness requires non-trivial integration work.
- Build the OpenHands or OpenCode wrappers without confirming they are in scope.
- Re-run the test-17 \texttt{deepseek-v4-flash} cells (we already have 3 seeds; re-running burns budget for no new information).
